title: galium rubrum subsp. corsicum
intro: Galium rubrum subsp. corsicum, also known as Corsican bedstraw, is a subspecies of the Galium rubrum plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Corsican bedstraw include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
